events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes DP impulse lines sloped wrong for service - gas taps below pipe / liquid taps on top. This is a classic installation error. For gas service, taps should be on top; for liquid service, taps should be on the bottom/side. The scenario says gas taps below pipe and liquid taps on top - both wrong.

This is non-compliant with API RP 551 which covers process measurement installation. The impulse lines must be sloped correctly to prevent accumulation of condensate (in gas) or gas bubbles (in liquid) in the impulse lines, which would cause measurement errors.

Decision: fail
Risk: high - incorrect impulse line sloping can cause measurement errors, leading to process control issues
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: ~70 (roughed in but not closed out)
Confidence: high

Let me write the JSON.

    "Installation does not comply with API RP 551 impulse piping requirements for process measurement"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reconfigure impulse piping so gas service taps are taken from the top of the pipe and lines slope downward toward the transmitter to drain condensate",
    "Reconfigure liquid service taps to the side or bottom of the pipe with lines sloped to prevent gas accumulation",
    "Verify final slope and tap orientation against API RP 551 and transmitter manufacturer installation requirements before closeout",
    "Do not proceed to testing or commissioning until impulse piping is corrected"
  ],
  "rationale": "API RP 551 requires gas service impulse lines to be taken from the top of the process line and sloped to prevent liquid accumulation, while liquid service lines must avoid trapping gas. The observed installation reverses both conventions, which will produce unreliable DP measurement and potential process control errors. Since the work is rough-complete but not yet closed out, it must be reworked before acceptance.",
  "references": ["API RP 551", "Manufacturer installation manuals"]
